message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also prompts.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or sequencing.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters support to prompt decorator (filters param, create_jinja_env helper). This is the only file my feature touches.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: We both need to touch prompts.py and it can't be split into disjoint file-level ownership since it's one file. Proposal: sequence instead of split — you implement the filters param + create_jinja_env helper first (foundational), send DONE with a summary of the new function signatures/helper name. Then I'll layer register_filter()/unregister_filter() on top, wiring the global registry into create_jinja_env so global filters merge with local ones (local takes precedence). I won't touch your code besides that integration point. Reply ACCEPT if that order works, then go ahead and edit — I'll wait for your DONE before I start.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Same file, different regions. I will touch ONLY: the  decorator signature/body, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, _template_from_str, _template_from_file, plus a new create_jinja_env() helper (placed near top, after imports/before Prompt class). Please add your register_filter/unregister_filter functions and any global registry variable as new standalone code, e.g. appended near the end of the file after parse_pydantic_schema, without editing the functions/methods I listed. That keeps our diffs non-overlapping. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed: I implement filters param on prompt/from_str/from_file plus create_jinja_env(extra_filters=None) helper first, then send DONE with exact signatures. You then add register_filter/unregister_filter and wire the global registry into create_jinja_env (local overrides global). Starting now.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Almost — one dependency: my feature requires that whatever calls create_jinja_env merges in a global filter registry (global filters available automatically, local ones taking precedence). Proposal: I'll define a module-level dict GLOBAL_FILTERS = {} plus register_filter()/unregister_filter(), appended near the end of the file as you suggested. Inside your create_jinja_env(filters), please add one line merging it in, e.g.: filters = {**GLOBAL_FILTERS, **(filters or {})} before building the Environment — that's the only reference to my code you'd need. I won't touch the decorator/from_str/from_file/_template_from_str/_template_from_file/create_jinja_env bodies beyond that agreed one-line contract, which you'll add yourself. Works?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Confirmed. I'll wait for your DONE message with exact signatures before touching the file. Once you're done, I'll add register_filter()/unregister_filter()/GLOBAL_FILTERS near the end of the file, and make a minimal one-line edit inside create_jinja_env to merge GLOBAL_FILTERS with local filters (local wins). Won't touch decorator/from_str/from_file bodies. Go ahead and start.

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Implemented: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment, registers builtin filters then env.filters.update(filters) if filters is not None (so passed-in filters override builtins). prompt(fn=None, *, filters=None) now supports @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}); internally calls Prompt._template_from_str(docstring, filters). Prompt.from_str(content, filters=None) and Prompt.from_file(path, filters=None) both accept filters and pass to _template_from_str/_template_from_file, which now just call create_jinja_env(loader, filters). All existing tests pass (13/13). Go ahead and wire your global registry into create_jinja_env - e.g. merge global registry dict first, then apply the local  arg on top so local overrides global, keeping current override order (any override of a builtin name) intact.

type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Concern: our patches are each graded standalone, the evaluator only reads my own patch.txt applied to a pristine repo, so I cannot reference GLOBAL_FILTERS in my copy since that name does not exist there - it would break my submission with a NameError. Instead: create_jinja_env(loader, filters) already accepts a plain dict and does filters-override-builtins via env.filters.update(filters). That dict parameter is the integration point. In your own patch, when you add register_filter/unregister_filter plus your global registry, also add your own merge step inside your own copy of create_jinja_env or the template methods, so your patch stays self-contained too. Both patches stay independently correct even though the diffs touch similar lines. Sound OK?

type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Note: my working copy of prompts.py never showed your create_jinja_env/filters-param changes (git status was clean before I started) -- looks like we're in separate sandboxes despite the shared-looking path. I went ahead and implemented the global filter registry standalone against the original file: added GLOBAL_FILTERS dict, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name), all appended after parse_pydantic_schema as agreed, plus one-line env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTERS) merges inside _template_from_str and _template_from_file (applied after the builtin filters, before your local-filters override would apply, so local still wins over global). All 13 existing tests pass. Wrote my diff to patch.txt. Good luck with your part!
